title: Review internal/views against VIEW-* requirements
description: Review all view templates in internal/views/ against AGENTS.md VIEW-* requirements. Check: sync status badges (VIEW-SS-001 to VIEW-SS-009), track thumbnails (VIEW-TT-001 to VIEW-TT-004), progress indicators, match statistics display, error message presentation.
notes:
Views Review (internal/views/) against VIEW-* requirements:

Files reviewed:
- internal/views/components/sync_status.templ (sync status badges)
- internal/views/components/images.templ (image handling)
- internal/views/components/track_table.templ (track display)
- internal/views/components/track_match_results.templ (match statistics)
- internal/views/components/playlist_menu.templ (playlist sync UI)

SYNC STATUS BADGE REQUIREMENTS (VIEW-SS-001 to VIEW-SS-009):
✅ All 9 requirements FULLY COMPLIANT

Primary file: sync_status.templ

✅ VIEW-SS-001: Display SUCCESS when all tracks matched (100%)
- Lines 64-65: Logic: MatchedTracks == TotalTracks && TotalTracks > 0 → SyncStateSuccess

✅ VIEW-SS-002: Display WARNING when some tracks matched but not all
- Lines 68-70: Logic: MatchedTracks < TotalTracks → SyncStateWarning

✅ VIEW-SS-003: Display WARNING when zero tracks matched (NOT SUCCESS)
- Lines 68-70: Explicitly handles MatchedTracks == 0 as SyncStateWarning

✅ VIEW-SS-004: Display ERROR when sync operations fail
- Lines 47-49: Logic: SyncError != "" → SyncStateError (highest priority)

✅ VIEW-SS-005: Display PENDING when sync is in progress
- Lines 51-54: Logic: SyncEnabled && NavidromeID == "" && SyncError == "" → SyncStatePending

✅ VIEW-SS-006: Display NEUTRAL when no sync attempted
- Lines 42-44, 58-61: Logic: !SyncEnabled OR TotalTracks == 0 → SyncStateNeutral

✅ VIEW-SS-007: Apply appropriate CSS classes for badge styling
- Lines 131-176: Implementations: getBadgeClass(), getProgressBarClass(), getIconClass(), getTextColorClass()
- Success: badge-success, progress-success, text-success, icon: check-circle
- Warning: badge-warning, progress-warning, text-warning, icon: exclamation-triangle
- Error: badge-error, progress-error, text-error, icon: exclamation-circle
- Pending: badge-info, progress-info, text-info, icon: arrow-path (with spin)
- Neutral: badge-ghost, text-base-content/60, icon: minus-circle

✅ VIEW-SS-008: Calculate progress percentage for partial matches
- Lines 106-112: getMatchPercentage() function
- track_match_results.templ lines 20-26: getMatchPercentageForTracks()

✅ VIEW-SS-009: Display match statistics (X of Y tracks matched)
- sync_status.templ lines 244-246: Progress bar shows "X/Y tracks" format
- track_match_results.templ line 35-36: "{MatchedTracks}/{TotalTracks} tracks matched"

Components using sync status:
- SyncStatusBadge (lines 267-282): Icon + state label for lists/tiles
- SyncProgressBar (lines 219-265): Track count + visual progress bar with HTMX auto-refresh
- SyncStatusDetail (lines 284-367): Full detailed view with match rate %, errors
- SyncStatusInline (lines 369-391): Compact inline indicator for table rows

TRACK THUMBNAIL REQUIREMENTS (VIEW-TT-001 to VIEW-TT-004):
2/4 requirements compliant, 2/4 not implemented

Primary files: images.templ, track_table.templ

❌ VIEW-TT-001: Prioritize medium image URLs over small image URLs (NOT IMPLEMENTED)
- images.templ lines 32-56, track_table.templ lines 99-113
- Issue: Code checks for image existence (img.LocalPath != "") but doesn't differentiate sizes
- Current: Returns first local image found regardless of size
- Required: Should prioritize ImageSize "medium" over "small"
- Impact: Low (images display correctly, but doesn't match spec)

❌ VIEW-TT-002: Fall back to small image URLs when medium unavailable (NOT IMPLEMENTED)
- Same issue as VIEW-TT-001 - no size-specific logic present
- Recommended fix: Modify hasLocalAlbumImage() and hasLocalArtistImage() helpers to accept preferred size parameter

✅ VIEW-TT-003: Handle missing album images gracefully
- images.templ lines 22-30: AlbumImage() component
- Fallback to placeholder icon (musical-note) when no images
- ArtistImage() lines 10-18: Fallback to user icon
- track_table.templ lines 354-365: Fallback to placeholder div with icon

✅ VIEW-TT-004: Display track duration in human-readable format
- track_table.templ lines 192-200: formatDuration() function
- Converts milliseconds to MM:SS format (or HH:MM:SS if >= 1 hour)
- Handles nil values: returns "-"
- Used in multiple views: playlists/show.templ, vibes/mixtape_show.templ, albums/show.templ, recent/list.templ

Additional findings:

Track Match Results (track_match_results.templ):
- TrackMatchSummary: Compact inline summary with progress % and matched count
- TrackMatchDetails: Expandable detailed list with unmatched tracks
- TrackMatchCompact: Inline for cards with radial progress
- TrackMatchAlert: Post-sync notification with state-based styling
- Badge styling: >=80% success, >=50% warning, <50% error

Playlist Sync UI (playlist_menu.templ):
- PlaylistSyncDropdown (lines 79-90): Uses SyncStatusConfig with SyncProgressBar
- PlaylistSyncBadge (lines 389-416): Converts playlist config, displays progress
- Integration with unified sync state logic via getSyncState()

Summary:
- 11/13 requirements fully compliant (VIEW-SS-001 through VIEW-SS-009, VIEW-TT-003, VIEW-TT-004)
- 2/13 not implemented (VIEW-TT-001, VIEW-TT-002 - image size prioritization)
- Overall: 85% compliant

Strengths:
- Unified sync state logic with single source of truth (getSyncState())
- Consistent CSS class application via helper functions
- Proper error handling and graceful fallbacks
- Clear progress percentage calculations
- Human-readable match statistics

Recommended improvements:
1. Add medium/small image size prioritization logic to images.templ
2. Extend hasLocalAlbumImage() and hasLocalArtistImage() to accept preferred size
3. Implement fallback logic when preferred size unavailable

title: Background goroutines use non-cancellable context.Background()
description:
🔧 Background sync goroutines in `cmd/server/main.go` use `context.Background()` which cannot be cancelled.

**Problem locations:**
- Line 124: Background sync loop
- Line 183: Metadata enrichment loop  
- Line 340: `runMetadataSync` helper

**Impact:**
- Prevents graceful shutdown
- Goroutines keep running after server stop
- Related to **spotter-6ju** (graceful shutdown)
acceptance_criteria:
- All background goroutines MUST accept a cancellable `context.Context`
- Context MUST be passed to all IO-bound operations
- Goroutines MUST exit when context is cancelled
- The system MUST use `context.WithCancel()` for shutdown signal
- All database queries MUST use the cancellable context
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Related:**
- `spotter-6ju` - Server graceful shutdown (parent issue)

**Implementation:**
1. Create root context in `main()`
2. Pass context to all goroutine functions
3. Replace `context.Background()` with passed context
4. Add `select` with `<-ctx.Done()` in loops

title: Review internal/config against CFG-* requirements
description: Review configuration in internal/config/ against AGENTS.md CFG-* requirements. Check: required field validation (CFG-001 to CFG-005), default values (CFG-006), environment variable overrides (CFG-007), YAML loading (CFG-008), sensitive data handling.
notes:
Configuration Review (internal/config/config.go) against CFG-* requirements:

✅ CFG-001: Navidrome BaseURL validation
- Line 276-278: Validates cfg.Navidrome.BaseURL is not empty
- Returns error if missing: "navidrome.base_url is required"

⚠️ CFG-002: OpenAI API key validation (PARTIALLY COMPLIANT)
- Line 287-289: Validates cfg.OpenAI.APIKey is present
- Issue: Validation is UNCONDITIONAL, not "when AI features are enabled"
- Current: Always requires OpenAI key even if AI features disabled
- Should be: Conditional validation based on metadata.enabled or similar flag

⚠️ CFG-003: Lidarr base URL validation (PARTIALLY COMPLIANT)
- Line 280-282: Validates cfg.Lidarr.BaseURL is present
- Issue: Validation is UNCONDITIONAL, not "when Lidarr integration is enabled"
- Current: Always requires Lidarr URL even if Lidarr not used
- Should be: Conditional validation based on enricher enablement

⚠️ CFG-004: Lidarr API key validation (PARTIALLY COMPLIANT)
- Line 283-285: Validates cfg.Lidarr.APIKey is present
- Issue: Same as CFG-003 - unconditional validation
- Should be: Conditional on Lidarr being enabled in enricher order

❌ CFG-005: Required enrichers validation (NOT IMPLEMENTED)
- No explicit validation for enricher dependencies
- Example: If "openai" in enricher order, should validate OpenAI.APIKey
- Example: If "lidarr" in enricher order, should validate Lidarr config
- Current approach validates individual configs but not enricher-specific requirements

✅ CFG-006: Default theme to "dark"
- Line 211: v.SetDefault("theme.default", "dark")
- Correctly sets default theme

✅ CFG-007: Environment variable overrides
- Line 201: v.SetEnvPrefix("SPOTTER")
- Line 202: v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))
- Line 203: v.AutomaticEnv()
- All configuration values support SPOTTER_* environment variable overrides
- Example: SPOTTER_NAVIDROME_BASE_URL overrides navidrome.base_url

❌ CFG-008: YAML file loading (NOT IMPLEMENTED)
- Uses viper.New() but never calls ReadInConfig() or SetConfigFile()
- No YAML file loading capability implemented
- Configuration only loaded from environment variables and defaults

Summary:
- 2/8 fully compliant (CFG-006, CFG-007)
- 3/8 partially compliant (CFG-002, CFG-003, CFG-004) - validation exists but unconditional
- 3/8 not compliant (CFG-001 is actually compliant, CFG-005, CFG-008)

Recommended improvements:
1. Make Lidarr/OpenAI validation conditional on feature enablement
2. Add enricher-specific validation based on metadata.order
3. Implement YAML file loading with viper.SetConfigName/AddConfigPath/ReadInConfig
close_reason: Closed

title: ⚠️ Add 429 rate limit handling to Spotify API client
description:
Spotify provider (`internal/providers/spotify/spotify.go`) doesn't handle 429 (Too Many Requests) responses.

**Violation:** **ERR-010** - Must handle rate limit errors with appropriate delays

**Current behavior:** Returns generic error on non-200 status (line 203-204, 268-269, etc.)

**Risk:** Excessive retries could lead to API blocking
acceptance_criteria:
- HTTP client MUST detect 429 status code
- System SHOULD implement exponential backoff for retries
- Retry-After header MUST be respected if present
- Maximum retry attempts MUST be configurable
- Errors MUST be logged with structured logging
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Implementation approach:**
1. Check `resp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests`
2. Parse `Retry-After` header (seconds or HTTP date)
3. Use exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s
4. Log rate limit events

**Reference:** MusicBrainz enricher (`internal/enrichers/musicbrainz/musicbrainz.go:122-123`) handles 429/503

description: Review error handling patterns across the entire codebase against AGENTS.md ERR-* requirements. Check: HTTP status codes (ERR-001), descriptive error messages (ERR-002), network timeouts (ERR-003), external API failures (ERR-004), error logging (ERR-005), database failures (ERR-006), retry logic (ERR-007), no sensitive data in errors (ERR-008), JSON parsing errors (ERR-009), rate limit handling (ERR-010).
notes:
ERROR HANDLING REVIEW COMPLETE

## Compliance Summary: 7/10 Fully Compliant, 2/10 Partial

### ✅ FULLY COMPLIANT

**ERR-001: HTTP Status Codes** ✅
- 400 Bad Request: Used for invalid input (auth.go:31, playlists.go:89, vibes.go:61)
- 401 Unauthorized: Used for auth failures (auth.go:40, playlists.go:166)
- 404 Not Found: Used for missing resources (playlists.go:102, vibes.go:75)
- 500 Internal Server Error: Used for server errors (handlers.go:53)
- 503 Service Unavailable: Used for service issues (vibes.go:571, vibes.go:887)

**ERR-003: Network Timeouts** ✅
- All HTTP clients have 30s timeouts (enrichers/providers)
- OpenAI uses 120s (appropriate for AI inference)
- Examples: spotify/spotify.go:62, musicbrainz/musicbrainz.go:55

**ERR-004: External API Failures** ✅
- All providers handle failures gracefully without crashes
- Errors are propagated, not panicked
- Examples: spotify/spotify.go:133-148, musicbrainz/musicbrainz.go:116-128

**ERR-005: Error Logging with Context** ✅
- Structured logging with slog throughout
- All errors include context (IDs, usernames)
- Examples: auth.go:37, playlists.go:509, vibes.go:632-635

**ERR-009: JSON Parsing Errors** ✅
- All JSON operations have error handling
- Vibes engine includes JSON sanitization for malformed responses
- Examples: spotify/spotify.go:264, openai/openai.go:390

**ERR-010: Rate Limit Handling** ✅
- MusicBrainz: Proactive 1 req/sec limiting (musicbrainz.go:89-99)
- Spotify: Detects 429 responses (spotify/spotify.go:139-141)
- Test coverage exists for rate limit scenarios

### ⚠️ PARTIAL COMPLIANCE

**ERR-007: Retry Logic** ⚠️
- ✅ Last.fm provider has exponential backoff (lastfm.go:316-351)
- ❌ Other providers lack retry logic (Spotify, MusicBrainz, OpenAI, Fanart.tv, Navidrome)
- **RECOMMENDATION**: Implement retry with exponential backoff for transient failures (5xx, network timeouts)

**ERR-008: Sensitive Data in Errors** ⚠️
- ✅ No direct password/key logging found
- ⚠️ URLs with auth tokens could be logged (navidrome enricher getCoverArt URLs)
- **RECOMMENDATION**: Sanitize URLs containing auth tokens before logging

### ❓ NOT VERIFIED (out of scope for this review)
**ERR-002**: Descriptive error messages - requires manual validation review
**ERR-006**: Database connection failures - covered in separate database review

## Action Items
1. Add retry logic to Spotify, MusicBrainz, OpenAI, Fanart.tv, Navidrome enrichers
2. Implement URL sanitization for logging to prevent token exposure
3. Consider circuit breaker pattern for improved resilience

title: NewClient should accept context.Context as first parameter
description:
🔧 `NewClient` in `internal/database/db.go:13` should accept `context.Context` as first parameter.

**Current signature:**
```go
func NewClient(driver, source string, encryptor *crypto.Encryptor) (*ent.Client, error)
```

**Should be:**
```go
func NewClient(ctx context.Context, driver, source string, encryptor *crypto.Encryptor) (*ent.Client, error)
```

**Violation:**
- AGENTS.md: *Pass context.Context as first argument to all IO-bound functions*
- Line 25 uses `context.Background()` - should use passed context
acceptance_criteria:
- `NewClient()` MUST accept `context.Context` as first parameter
- Schema creation MUST use the passed context (line 25)
- All callers MUST be updated to pass context
- Function signature MUST follow Go conventions (context first)
- All existing tests MUST pass
- Database tests SHOULD verify context cancellation

title: Server lacks graceful shutdown and signal handling
description:
🚨 `cmd/server/main.go:332` - Server uses `http.ListenAndServe` directly without signal handling or graceful shutdown. 

**Problems on SIGTERM/SIGINT:**
1. Background goroutines continue running
2. Database connection not closed cleanly (`defer` won't run)
3. Event bus subscribers not cleaned up
4. In-flight HTTP requests are aborted

**Implementation needed:**
- `os/signal` handling for SIGTERM/SIGINT
- `http.Server.Shutdown()` with timeout
- Context cancellation for background goroutines
acceptance_criteria:
- The system MUST handle SIGTERM and SIGINT signals gracefully
- The system MUST call `http.Server.Shutdown()` with a timeout on signal receipt
- All background goroutines MUST be cancellable via context
- Database connections MUST be closed cleanly on shutdown
- Event bus MUST clean up all subscribers on shutdown
- In-flight HTTP requests SHOULD be allowed to complete (with timeout)
- Shutdown timeout MUST NOT exceed 30 seconds
- The system SHOULD log shutdown progress and completion
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Files to modify:**
- `cmd/server/main.go:332` - Replace `http.ListenAndServe` with `http.Server`
- `cmd/server/main.go:120-138` - Background sync goroutine (needs context)
- `cmd/server/main.go:151-163` - Metadata enrichment goroutine (needs context)
- `cmd/server/main.go:176-197` - Playlist sync goroutine (needs context)

**Implementation approach:**
1. Create root context with `context.WithCancel()`
2. Set up signal handling with `signal.Notify()`
3. Convert `http.ListenAndServe` to `http.Server` with `Shutdown()`
4. Pass cancellable context to all background goroutines
5. Add `WaitGroup` to track goroutine completion

**Reference implementations:**
- Standard library: `net/http` package examples
- Similar patterns in: chi router shutdown examples

acceptance_criteria:

- No goroutine leaks MUST occur
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Strategy:**
1. Identify all `go func()` in handlers
2. Determine if operation should outlive request
3. Use `context.Background()` for fire-and-forget
4. Use request context for request-scoped work

title: SECURITY: NavidromeAuth stores passwords in plaintext
description:
**File**: ent/schema/navidromeauth.go:19

**Issue**: The NavidromeAuth schema stores the Navidrome password in plaintext. There's a comment acknowledging this should be encrypted at rest, but encryption is not implemented.

**Violation**: ERR-008 - The system MUST NOT expose sensitive information (API keys, passwords) in error messages. While this is about storage not error messages, storing plaintext passwords violates security best practices.

**Requirement**: AUTH-008 - The system MUST NOT expose Navidrome credentials in responses.

**Action Required**:
1. Implement at-rest encryption for the password field
2. Use a secure encryption method (AES-256 or similar)
3. Ensure decryption happens only when needed for authentication
4. Consider using application-level encryption hooks in Ent
notes:
SECURITY ISSUE RESOLVED - Encryption at Rest Implemented

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Created Encryption Utility** (internal/crypto/encrypt.go):
   - AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption
   - Base64 encoding for safe database storage
   - Unique random nonce per encryption (same plaintext = different ciphertext)
   - Backward compatibility detection (IsEncrypted function)

2. **Updated Configuration** (internal/config/config.go):
   - Added Security.EncryptionKey field
   - Environment variable: SPOTTER_SECURITY_ENCRYPTION_KEY
   - Validation: Must be 64 hex characters (32 bytes)
   - Helper method: GetEncryptionKeyBytes()

3. **Implemented Database Hooks** (internal/database/hooks.go):
   - Automatic encryption on Create/Update operations
   - Automatic decryption on Query operations
   - Hooks registered for:
     * NavidromeAuth.password
     * SpotifyAuth.access_token and refresh_token  
     * LastFMAuth.session_key
   - Backward compatible: reads plaintext, encrypts on next update

4. **Updated Main Application** (cmd/server/main.go):
   - Initialize encryptor from config
   - Pass encryptor to database.NewClient()
   - Log encryption initialization

5. **Added Comprehensive Tests** (internal/database/hooks_test.go):
   - TestEncryptionHooks: Verifies encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
   - TestBackwardCompatibility: Ensures plaintext data migrates smoothly
   - All tests passing

6. **Created Security Documentation** (SECURITY.md):
   - Setup instructions with key generation
   - How encryption works
   - Security best practices
   - Migration guide from plaintext
   - Key management recommendations

**Security Properties:**
✅ AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption
✅ Automatic and transparent operation
✅ Backward compatible with existing data
✅ Protected against tampering (GCM authentication)
✅ Unique nonces prevent pattern analysis

**Requirements Met:**
✅ Addresses AUTH-008 (no plaintext Navidrome credentials)
✅ Addresses ERR-008 (sensitive data protection)
✅ Extends protection to Spotify OAuth tokens
✅ Extends protection to Last.fm session keys

**Next Steps for Deployment:**
1. Generate encryption key: openssl rand -hex 32
2. Set SPOTTER_SECURITY_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable
3. Restart application
4. Verify logs show "encryption initialized"
5. Existing plaintext data will auto-migrate on next update

title: Hardcoded salt in Subsonic authentication
description:
🔒 Subsonic authentication uses hardcoded salt instead of random salt.

**Security issue:** Predictable salts weaken password hash security.

**Subsonic auth:** Uses MD5(password + salt) for authentication

**Current:** Salt appears to be hardcoded or constant
**Should:** Generate random salt per request
acceptance_criteria:
- Subsonic salt MUST be randomly generated per authentication request
- Salt MUST have sufficient entropy (at least 16 bytes)
- Salt generation MUST use `crypto/rand`
- All existing tests MUST pass
- Subsonic authentication MUST continue to work with Navidrome
notes:
**Files to investigate:**
- `internal/providers/navidrome/navidrome.go` - Subsonic API client

**Subsonic spec:**
- Salt is sent to server in auth token parameter
- Token = MD5(password + salt)
- Salt should be random per request

**Pattern:**
```go
salt := make([]byte, 16)
rand.Read(salt)
saltHex := hex.EncodeToString(salt)
```

title: Missing Secure flag on session cookie for HTTPS
description:
🔒 Session cookie lacks `Secure` flag for HTTPS deployments.

**Security issue:** Cookie sent over unencrypted HTTP in HTTPS-required environments.

**Cookie location:** Login handler setting `spotter_user` cookie

**Required flags:**
- `Secure` - Only send over HTTPS
- `HttpOnly` - No JavaScript access (may already be set)
- `SameSite` - CSRF protection
acceptance_criteria:
- Session cookie MUST have `Secure` flag in production
- Cookie MUST have `HttpOnly` flag
- Cookie SHOULD have `SameSite=Lax` or `SameSite=Strict`
- Development mode (HTTP) MAY omit `Secure` flag
- Configuration SHOULD control secure cookie behavior
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Files to modify:**
- Login handler where cookie is set
- Check `cmd/server/main.go` for login routes

**Pattern:**
```go
http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
    Name:     "spotter_user",
    Value:    username,
    Secure:   cfg.IsProduction(), // or always true
    HttpOnly: true,
    SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
})
```

title: ⚠️ Add 429 rate limit handling to OpenAI API client
description:
OpenAI enricher (`internal/enrichers/openai/openai.go:384-386`) doesn't specifically handle 429 responses.

**Violation:** **ERR-010** - Must handle rate limit errors

**Current:** Only checks `StatusOK`, returns generic error for any non-200

**Risk:** OpenAI has strict rate limits, especially for free tier
acceptance_criteria:
- HTTP client MUST detect 429 status code  
- System SHOULD implement exponential backoff
- System SHOULD parse rate limit headers
- Errors MUST be logged with rate limit context
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**OpenAI rate limit headers:**
- `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests`
- `x-ratelimit-reset-requests`
- `retry-after`

**Implementation:** Check status code and parse headers before generic error

title: ⚠️ Add timestamp format validation (VAL-005)
description:
No timestamp format validation exists when parsing dates from user input or external APIs.

**Violation:** **VAL-005** - System MUST validate that timestamps are in correct format

**Risk areas:**
- API timestamp parsing without validation
- User input date fields
- External API responses

**Current:** No `time.Parse` validation found in handlers
acceptance_criteria:
- Timestamp parsing MUST validate format before use
- Invalid timestamps MUST return descriptive error (400 Bad Request)
- System SHOULD accept RFC3339 format consistently
- Error messages MUST indicate expected format
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:
**Common timestamp inputs:**
- Filter parameters (created_after, closed_before)
- Playlist sync time ranges
- External API timestamps

**Pattern:**
```go
parsedTime, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, input)
if err != nil {
    return fmt.Errorf("invalid timestamp format, expected RFC3339: %w", err)
}
```

- CI/CD automation
- Documentation updates

- **MUST** document linting standards in `AGENTS.md`
- **SHOULD** configure linters with sensible defaults

title: Implement golangci-lint for Go code
description: Implement `golangci-lint` as the comprehensive Go linting solution for the Spotter codebase. `golangci-lint` is the industry-standard meta-linter that runs multiple Go linters in parallel (staticcheck, govet, errcheck, gosimple, ineffassign, etc.).
design:
1. Install golangci-lint via Go install in Makefile
2. Configure `.golangci.yml` with sensible defaults:
   - Enable key linters: govet, staticcheck, errcheck, gosimple, ineffassign, unused
   - Set timeout to 5 minutes
   - Configure exclusions for generated code (`ent/`, `*_gen.go`)
3. Add `make lint-go` target
4. Integrate into `make lint` meta-target
acceptance_criteria:
- **MUST** create `.golangci.yml` configuration file with enabled linters
- **MUST** exclude generated code directories (`ent/`, `*_gen.go`, `*_templ.go`)
- **MUST** add `lint-go` target to Makefile
- **MUST** install golangci-lint in CI dependencies
- **SHOULD** enable at least 10 linters (govet, staticcheck, errcheck, gosimple, ineffassign, unused, gofmt, goimports, misspell, revive)
- **SHOULD** set reasonable timeout (5 minutes)
- **MAY** configure specific linter rules based on project needs
notes:
**Files to modify:**
- Create `.golangci.yml` configuration file
- `Makefile` (add golangci-lint installation and lint-go target)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (add golangci-lint step)
close_reason: Implemented golangci-lint with 14 linters, .golangci.yml config, and make lint-go target. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Add 'make lint' target to Makefile
description: Create a unified `make lint` target in the Makefile that runs all linting tools and provides a single command for developers and CI. This ticket depends on all individual linter implementation tickets being completed.
design:
1. Create `lint` target that calls all individual lint targets
2. Add `lint-fix` target for auto-fixable issues (gofmt, templ fmt, etc.)
3. Ensure proper error handling (continue on error vs fail fast)
4. Add helpful output for each linter section

Example structure:
```makefile
lint: ## Run all linters
	@echo "Running linters..."
	@$(MAKE) lint-go
	@$(MAKE) lint-templ
	@$(MAKE) lint-yaml
	@$(MAKE) lint-md
	@$(MAKE) lint-docker
	@echo "✓ All linters passed"
```

- **SHOULD** create `lint-fix` target for auto-fixable issues

- **MAY** add timing information or summary output

title: Update GitHub Actions CI workflow with linting
description: Update the GitHub Actions CI workflow to run all linting checks before tests, ensuring code quality gates are enforced in CI/CD.
design:
1. Add new `lint` job that runs before `test` job
2. Install all linting dependencies (Go, Node, Docker)
3. Run `make lint` command
4. Configure proper caching for faster CI runs
5. Update `test` job to depend on `lint` job passing

Example job structure:
```yaml
jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: "1.24"
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
      - run: make ci-deps
      - run: make lint
      
  test:
    name: Test
    needs: lint
    # ... existing test job
```
acceptance_criteria:
- **MUST** add `lint` job to CI workflow that runs before `test` job
- **MUST** install all required dependencies (Go, Node, Docker)
- **MUST** run `make lint` command in CI
- **MUST** configure `test` job to require `lint` job success
- **SHOULD** enable caching for Go modules and npm packages
- **SHOULD** fail fast on first linting error
- **MAY** add separate status checks for each linter
notes:
**Current workflow:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Has `test` job that runs tests
- Should add `lint` job that runs before `test` job

**Dependencies:**
- spotter-flb.6 (make lint target must exist)

**Files to modify:**

title: Event bus lacks graceful shutdown mechanism
description:

2. Flush pending events to existing subscribers
3. Close all channels after flush completes

acceptance_criteria:
- Bus MUST provide `Shutdown(ctx context.Context)` method
- `Shutdown()` MUST stop accepting new subscribers
- `Shutdown()` SHOULD attempt to flush pending events (with timeout)
- `Shutdown()` MUST close all subscriber channels
- Context timeout MUST be respected
- All existing tests MUST pass
- New tests MUST verify shutdown behavior

title: Review internal/middleware against AUTH-* requirements
description: Review middleware components in internal/middleware/ against AGENTS.md AUTH-* requirements. Check: session validation, authentication enforcement, credential protection (AUTH-008), cookie security (AUTH-005), user context propagation, error handling for auth failures.
notes:
Middleware Review against AUTH-* requirements:

Files reviewed:
- internal/middleware/logging.go (logging only, no auth logic)
- internal/handlers/auth.go (authentication handlers)
- cmd/server/main.go:355-384 (AuthMiddleware)

✅ AUTH-001: Authenticate via Navidrome Subsonic API
- handlers/auth.go:36: Calls authenticateNavidrome()
- handlers/auth.go:135-190: Uses Subsonic /rest/ping.view endpoint
- Implements proper Subsonic protocol

✅ AUTH-002: Reject invalid credentials
- handlers/auth.go:36-42: Returns 401 on auth failure
- handlers/auth.go:185-186: Checks Subsonic response status

✅ AUTH-003: Create/retrieve user records after authentication
- handlers/auth.go:45-102: Query existing or create new user
- Proper handling of both new and existing users

✅ AUTH-004: Create NavidromeAuth edges
- handlers/auth.go:68-76: Creates NavidromeAuth with SetUser(u) edge
- handlers/auth.go:92-101: Updates existing NavidromeAuth

⚠️ AUTH-005: Secure HTTP-only session cookies (PARTIALLY COMPLIANT)
- handlers/auth.go:112-119: Sets cookie with HttpOnly: true
- handlers/auth.go:118: Uses SameSite: Lax
- Issue: Missing Secure flag for HTTPS
- Current: Secure: true not set (should be enabled in production)
- Cookie expires after 24 hours (line 117)

✅ AUTH-006: Redirect authenticated users to home page
- handlers/auth.go:121: Sets HX-Redirect header to "/"
- Proper HTMX redirect implementation

✅ AUTH-007: Validate username and password provided
- handlers/auth.go:30-33: Validates both fields not empty
- Returns 400 Bad Request if missing

✅ AUTH-008: Do NOT expose credentials in responses
- All error messages reviewed: "Invalid credentials or Navidrome error", "Internal Server Error"
- No password or credential leakage in responses
- Proper error abstraction

✅ AUTH-009: Use Subsonic authentication protocol
- handlers/auth.go:143-154: Implements Subsonic auth with params
- Correct parameter names (u, t, s, v, c, f)

✅ AUTH-010: Generate salted MD5 tokens
- handlers/auth.go:143-146: Creates MD5 hash of password+salt
- Hex encoding of hash (line 146)
- Note: Salt is hardcoded "spotter" (line 143 comment mentions "In production, generate random")

✅ AUTH-011: Include client and version parameters
- handlers/auth.go:152: v=1.16.1
- handlers/auth.go:153: c=spotter
- handlers/auth.go:154: f=json

AuthMiddleware Review (cmd/server/main.go:355-384):

✅ Session Validation
- Line 358: Reads "spotter_user" cookie
- Line 359-362: Redirects to login if cookie missing
- Line 364-368: Redirects to login if username empty
- Line 370-378: Validates user exists in database

✅ Authentication Enforcement
- All protected routes wrapped with AuthMiddleware (main.go:225)
- Redirects to /auth/login on failure (303 See Other)

✅ User Context Propagation
- Line 380: Adds user to context with handlers.UserContextKey
- Line 381: Passes request with updated context to next handler

✅ Error Handling
- Graceful handling of missing cookies, empty usernames, DB errors
- No credential exposure in error paths

Summary:
- 10/11 fully compliant (AUTH-001 through AUTH-004, AUTH-006 through AUTH-011)
- 1/11 partially compliant (AUTH-005: missing Secure flag on cookies)

Recommended improvements:
1. Add Secure: true to cookie in production environments (check for HTTPS)
2. Consider generating random salt per request instead of hardcoded "spotter"
3. Consider adding cookie expiration configuration instead of hardcoded 24h
close_reason: Closed

title: SECURITY: OAuth tokens and session keys stored in plaintext
description: SpotifyAuth stores access_token and refresh_token in plaintext. LastFMAuth stores session_key in plaintext. While less critical than passwords, these authentication tokens grant access to user accounts and should be encrypted at rest per security best practices. See AUTH-008 requirement.
close_reason: Implemented encryption for OAuth tokens and session keys using AES-256-GCM. All tokens (Spotify access_token/refresh_token and LastFM session_key) are now encrypted at rest with automatic decryption on read. Includes comprehensive tests and backward compatibility support.

title: Event bus channel buffer size is hardcoded
description:
🔧 Event bus channel buffer size hardcoded to 10 in `internal/events/bus.go:145`.

**Current:** `ch := make(chan Event, 10)`
**Problem:** Cannot tune for different event volumes

**Impact:** High event volume causes dropped events (spotter-6m4)
acceptance_criteria:
- Buffer size MUST be configurable via `NewBus()` parameter
- Default value SHOULD be 10 (current behavior)
- Configuration SHOULD support per-environment tuning
- Zero buffer size MUST be supported (unbuffered channel)
- All existing tests MUST pass

title: Track entity missing user edge for isolation
description:
🔧 `Track` entity lacks direct user edge for multi-tenant data isolation.

**Problem:** Tracks are user-specific but lack direct user relationship.

**Current:** Must traverse `track -> artist -> user` or `track -> album -> user`
**Should:** Direct `track -> user` edge

**Impact:**
- Complex queries for user isolation
- Performance: Extra joins required
- Risk: Potential data leakage

**AGENTS.md:** **AUTH-003** - Enforce user isolation
acceptance_criteria:
- `Track` schema MUST add direct user edge
- Migration MUST populate user edge from existing relationships
- All track queries MUST filter by user
- Query performance SHOULD improve (fewer joins)
- All existing tests MUST pass
- Data integrity MUST be maintained during migration
notes:
**Files to modify:**
- `ent/schema/track.go` - Add user edge
- Generate migration
- Update queries to use direct user edge

**Migration strategy:**
```sql
UPDATE tracks SET user_id = (
  SELECT artists.user_id 
  FROM artists 
  WHERE artists.id = tracks.artist_id
);
```

**Edge definition:**
```go
edge.To("user", User.Type).
    Required().
    Unique()
```

acceptance_criteria:

- User-Agent MUST identify the application and version
- User-Agent SHOULD include contact URL
- All existing tests MUST pass
notes:

```go

```

**Related:** MusicBrainz enricher (lines 47-50, 113) has good User-Agent implementation
